response.justification: Wei Chen is a skilled full-stack engineer with 18+ years of development experience and recent exposure to MCP and agentic workflows. However, this role requires minimum 8 years building production-grade AI solutions—this candidate has only 2 months in an AI-adjacent infrastructure role at re:cinq. Critical gaps include no Python expertise (a core required language), no experience with vector databases, RAG architectures, or LLM optimization, and no data pipeline development background. While the current MCP work is relevant, the fundamental lack of AI systems delivery experience and the absence of required technical foundations make this unsuitable for a staff-level forward-deployed AI engineering position.
strengths: Recent hands-on work with MCP and agentic workflow frameworks at re:cinq demonstrates familiarity with modern AI tooling
Strong TypeScript and Node.js expertise with proven full-stack capabilities across multiple architecture domains
Established track record of staff-level technical leadership, mentoring, and knowledge-sharing across organizations
concerns: Zero production AI/ML systems experience despite role requiring minimum 8 years—current AI work spans only 2 months at re:cinq
No Python expertise, which is a core required language for AI solution development and listed among primary technologies
Missing fundamental AI technical foundation: no experience with vector databases, RAG architectures, LLM performance metrics, or data pipelines

key_factors.factor: Production AI experience gap (0 years vs 8+ required)
Recent MCP and agentic framework exposure
